name: Small Business Owner
description: Run the business and your own finances without dropping either. Domains for the company, cashflow, taxes, customers, and your calendar.

domains.title: Business
domains.soul:
# Business

The company: what it sells, who it serves, and the decisions that keep it healthy. Track revenue, runway, hiring, and the handful of bets that matter this quarter.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Keep at least 6 months of runway in the bank
- Know this quarter's top 3 priorities and protect time for them
- Review key metrics weekly, not when something breaks
domains.config:
# Config

business_name: 
entity_type: 
fiscal_year_end: 
key_metrics: revenue, runway, gross_margin
prompts: What are my top 3 business priorities this quarter and what is the next action on each?
Review my cashflow: what is my runway and what is the biggest risk to it?
Draft a weekly metrics review from the numbers in this domain.

domains.title: Cashflow & Wealth
domains.soul:
# Cashflow & Wealth

The money flowing through the business and into your own accounts. Separate the two cleanly; pay yourself deliberately; plan for taxes before they are due.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Pay yourself a consistent draw / salary
- Set aside taxes every month, not in April
- Build a personal emergency fund separate from the business
prompts: How much should I set aside for taxes this month based on revenue?
Am I paying myself sustainably given the business's runway?

domains.title: Taxes
domains.soul:
# Taxes

Everything tax: estimated payments, deadlines, deductible expenses, and the documents your accountant will ask for. Never miss a deadline; never overpay.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Make quarterly estimated payments on time
- Keep deductible expenses categorized as they happen
- Have clean books before year-end, not after
prompts: What tax deadlines are coming in the next 60 days and what do I owe?
Which of my recent expenses are likely deductible and how should I categorize them?

domains.title: Calendar
domains.soul:
# Calendar

Your time across the business and your life. Protect deep-work blocks, watch meeting load, and surface deadlines before they become emergencies.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Keep meeting load under 40% of working hours
- Protect at least two deep-work blocks a week
- See deadlines 7 days out, every week
prompts: Build my weekly preview: deadlines, meeting load, and one focus recommendation.
Where are the conflicts between business commitments and personal time this week?
